articles.title: These fans are boycotting the World Cup. Will they make it a bust?
articles.summary: Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are unhappy with World Cup ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they're deciding not to come, raising concerns across the travel industry.

articles.title: Oil Price Hits Wartime High Above $120 a Barrel as Iran War Standoff Continues
articles.summary: The longer the disruption to Middle East fuel supplies lasts, the risk grows that higher energy costs will feed into broader inflation that could dent economic growth.

articles.title: Behind Powell’s High-Stakes Decision to Stay at the Fed
articles.summary: Jerome H. Powell will remain a governor at the Federal Reserve after his term as chair ends, in a bid to guard against a further incursion by the Trump administration on the central bank’s independenc

articles.title: Rising Fuel Prices Force Policymakers to Weigh Excruciating Choices
articles.summary: The Bank of England and European Central Bank held interest rates steady on Thursday, as officials search for signs of possible longer-term damage and warn of the impact of a prolonged energy shock.

articles.title: U.S. Economy Grew 2 Percent in Early 2026 Even as War in Iran Began to Hit Energy Prices
articles.summary: Gross domestic product expanded at a 2 percent annual rate in the first three months of the year, a period including first weeks of conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: Why the U.A.E. Is Quitting OPEC
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates is walking away from OPEC this May. The New York Times’ energy reporter, Rebecca Elliott, breaks down how the war with Iran provided the perfect opening for the Emirates to go

articles.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
articles.summary: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

articles.title: What the Voting Rights Act Decision Means, and Hegseth’s Heated Testimony
articles.summary: Plus, chatbots told scientists how to make bioweapons.

articles.title: To catch colorectal cancer early, advocates push to make 'poop talk' OK
articles.summary: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.

articles.title: Iranians Live With Pain and Powerlessness, Beneath a Smooth Veneer
articles.summary: After months of upheaval, many are attempting to get on with their lives while quietly grappling with grief, economic stress and a loss of hope.

articles.title: How the War in Iran is Affecting Africa’s Economy and Fuel Supply
articles.summary: Despite the fragile cease-fire in the Middle East, many Africans say they are bracing for tougher times ahead and making difficult decisions about the future.

articles.title: The Iraq vet redefining mindfulness, one bourbon at a time
articles.summary: Like many vets, it took Fred Minnick time to find the best way to cope with what he would learn was PTSD. For Minnick, sense and peace came with bourbon — and "taste mindfulness."

articles.title: After lackluster tour turnout, Turning Point wraps things up with excited crowd in Idaho
articles.summary: The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.

articles.title: Supreme Court paves the way for largest-ever drop in Black representation in Congress
articles.summary: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.

articles.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastr

articles.title: Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
articles.summary: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.

articles.title: The U.S. economy shows resilience despite the war with Iran
articles.summary: The U.S. economy revved up in the first three months of the year, despite a spike in energy prices caused by the war with Iran.

articles.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
articles.summary: Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near

articles.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing American troop presence in Germany after Iran feud
articles.summary: President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.

articles.title: The Austrian nuns who fled their care home are now in Rome and visited the Vatican
articles.summary: The three octogenarian nuns, who made headlines last year after they broke back into their convent, joined others at St. Peter's Square for a general audience with Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday morning.

articles.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
articles.summary: The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end.

articles.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
articles.summary: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
